textPlain: It’s great to see JetBrains giving back like this …The Raku language community has put a lot of effort into IntelliJ support, initially the Comma plugin was supported by Edument and then it was passed to the Raku Community where it is actively supported … now renamed Raku IntelliJ Plugin (RIP - geddit?)Tools like the Grammar inspector and debugger fit well in the IntelliJ UI model.https://raku.org/nav/1/tools

Both Saif and Markus are excellent choices for sponsorships. Kudos to whoever picked them. It’s a shame Juliette is losing hers, she’s also a really valued member of the PHP community and has been for a long time.
I have a lot of respect for JetBrains.They are bootstrapped, doing $400M+ revenue, and selling to one of the hardest segments (developers who are notorious frugal).
